text: In software development, testing plays a crucial role in ensuring quality. **Unit tests** check individual pieces of code, like a single function or method. **Integration tests** then verify that these different units work together correctly. Many developers use **test-driven development**, where they write a failing test first, then code just enough to make it pass. This approach helps catch bugs early and clarifies what the code should do. Ultimately, a strong testing strategy builds **confidence**—allowing developers to make changes or add features without fear of breaking existing functionality.
